pageDescription: Calculate batting average and related statistics for baseball and softball players with comprehensive performance metrics

definition.title: Batting Average Definition
definition.description: Batting Average = Hits ÷ At Bats (does not include walks, sacrifices, or hit by pitch)

benchmarks.title: Performance Benchmarks
benchmarks.description: Excellent: .300+, Good: .270-.299, Average: .250-.269, Below Average: Below .250

context.title: Context Matters
context.description: Consider ballpark, opponents, and game situations when evaluating batting average

obp.title: On-Base Percentage (OBP)
obp.description: Measures how frequently a batter reaches base

slg.title: Slugging Percentage (SLG)
slg.description: Measures the power of a hitter

ops.title: On-Base Plus Slugging (OPS)
ops.description: Combines OBP and SLG for overall offensive performance

criticalWarningsList.notDiagnostic: This calculator provides statistical calculations only
criticalWarningsList.context: Batting average should be evaluated with other statistics for complete analysis

importantNotesList: Batting average doesn't account for walks, sacrifices, or hit by pitch
Consider sample size when evaluating recent performance
Different ballparks and leagues may affect performance metrics
Advanced metrics like OPS and wOBA provide more complete player evaluation

messages.enterInformation: Enter player statistics to calculate batting average
messages.resultMessage: The player's batting average is shown below

disclaimer.title: Important Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: This calculator provides batting average calculations based on standard baseball statistics formulas. Results are for informational purposes and may not reflect official league statistics or advanced analytical metrics.

seekHelp.title: Baseball Resources
seekHelp.content: For professional baseball analysis and advanced statistics, consult sports analysts, coaches, or specialized baseball statistical resources and sabermetric tools.

errors.atBats: Please enter a valid number of at bats (must be greater than 0)
errors.hits: Please enter a valid number of hits (cannot exceed at bats)
errors.playerName: Please enter a valid player name

tooltips.atBats: Number of official at bats (excludes walks, sacrifices, hit by pitch)
tooltips.hits: Number of hits (singles, doubles, triples, home runs)
tooltips.calculationType: Select the type of statistical calculation
tooltips.additionalStats: Include advanced statistics like OBP, SLG, and OPS

choose_between_basic_or_advanced_calcula: Choose between basic or advanced calculation methods
what_calculation_method_would_you_like_t: What calculation method would you like to use?
batting_average_runs_scored_times_out_9: Batting Average = Runs Scored ÷ Times Out
times_out_innings_played_not_outs_15: Times Out = Innings Played - Not Outs
batting_average_runs_scored_times_out_16: Batting Average = Runs Scored ÷ Times Out
not_out_innings_dont_count_as_dismissals: Not out innings don't count as dismissals, which can increase your average
enter_your_cricket_statistics_and_click_: Enter your cricket statistics and click
batting_average_in_cricket_is_a_statisti:
Batting average in cricket is a statistic that measures a batsman's performance. It represents
                        the average number of runs scored per dismissal.
higher_averages_indicate_better_batting_: Higher averages indicate better batting performance
not_out_innings_are_not_counted_as_dismi: Not out innings are not counted as dismissals
a_good_batting_average_varies_by_level_o: A good batting average varies by level of cricket
professional_averages_above_40_are_consi: Professional averages above 40 are considered excellent
this_would_be_an_exceptional_batting_ave:
This would be an exceptional batting average, indicating the player scores an average of 105
                        runs before getting out.
